# Claude Code Context Health Diagnostic Guide

> Identify and fix context degradation in Claude Code sessions. Covers 5 failure modes with symptoms, diagnostics, and mitigation strategies.

## The 5 Context Failure Modes

1. **Context Rot** — Early instructions/decisions compressed or forgotten. Claude stops following session-start rules.
2. **Context Pollution** — Irrelevant data (verbose errors, large file dumps) dilutes effective signal in the context window.
3. **Context Drift** — Gradual deviation from original task goal. Bug fix becomes module rewrite.
4. **Context Explosion** — Context window fills rapidly, triggers auto-compression, massive information loss.
5. **Context Cross-Contamination** — Different project contexts interfere. Project A patterns applied to Project B.

## Key Prevention Strategies

- One task per session (most effective)
- Persist critical decisions to CLAUDE.md + memory, don't rely on session memory
- Use subagents for exploratory work (their context stays isolated)
- Regular checkpoints — verify Claude still understands the big picture
- New sessions are cheap — when in doubt, start fresh

## Quick Health Check

- Ask Claude to restate all architecture decisions → can't recall = Context Rot
- Compare initial request vs current action → diverged = Context Drift  
- Check referenced paths/functions actually exist → phantom refs = Cross-Contamination
- Monitor git diff --stat → unexpected file count = Drift

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/MackDing/claude-context-health
- Author: https://github.com/MackDing
